message:
feat(freebsd): fix from-source build + drop stale submodule gitlink

Five coordinated fixes so `cargo build --release` works on fresh
clones, including on FreeBSD 15:

- Cargo.toml: add target-specific deps for `cfg(target_os = "freebsd")`.
  * rquickjs gets the `bindgen` feature — rquickjs-sys ships no pre-built
    FFI bindings for x86_64-unknown-freebsd, so we regenerate at compile
    time using /usr/bin/clang from the base system.
  * libsqlite3-sys switches to the `bundled` feature, mirroring the
    existing Windows workaround, so linking doesn't depend on sqlite3
    being discoverable at /usr/local/lib by rustc's default search path.

- .cargo/config.toml (new): add -L /usr/local/lib rustflag and
  PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/usr/local/libdata/pkgconfig under
  cfg(target_os = "freebsd"). Inert on Linux/macOS/Windows.

- legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono: remove the broken submodule gitlink
  (no .gitmodules, and SHA 10bca71f is unreachable in
  badlogic/pi-mono), so fresh clones don't land in an unpopulated
  submodule and fail Cargo's `include = [...]` check. The 6-line
  crates.io-publishing stub that already lives at the expected path
  is now tracked as a regular file, which is all the published crate
  needs — no need to vendor the 14k-line upstream
  `models.generated.ts` for a Rust crate that never reads it at
  runtime.

Addresses #52 (external report from @sahuagin).

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Platform-specific build configuration.
+#
+# FreeBSD installs third-party libraries (clang, openssl, sqlite, …) under
+# /usr/local via pkg(8). Rustc and pkg-config do not search there by default,
+# so we add the prefix to the native-library and pkg-config search paths.
+# These settings are inert on Linux/macOS/Windows.
+
+[target.'cfg(target_os = "freebsd")']
+rustflags = ["-L", "/usr/local/lib"]
+
+[env]
+PKG_CONFIG_PATH = { value = "/usr/local/libdata/pkgconfig", force = false }

@@ -170,6 +170,14 @@ tikv-jemallocator = { version = "0.6", optional = true }
 [target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies]
 libsqlite3-sys = { version = "0.37", features = ["bundled-windows"] }
 
+# FreeBSD: third-party libs live under /usr/local and rquickjs-sys doesn't
+# ship pre-generated bindings for this target, so regenerate at build time
+# via the `bindgen` feature (uses /usr/bin/clang from the base system).
+# Bundled sqlite mirrors the Windows workaround.
+[target.'cfg(target_os = "freebsd")'.dependencies]
+libsqlite3-sys = { version = "0.37", features = ["bundled"] }
+rquickjs = { version = "0.11", features = ["futures", "loader", "bindgen"] }
+
 [features]
 default = ["image-resize", "jemalloc", "clipboard", "wasm-host", "sqlite-sessions"]
 image-resize = ["image"]

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+// Auto-generated stub for crates.io publishing.
+// The real file lives in the pi-mono submodule.
+
+import type { Model } from "./types";
+
+export const MODELS = {} as const;
